prompt: Documentary photography style. A vast white empty plain stretches to every horizon. On the left side of the frame, hundreds of densely packed animal silhouettes fill the space — elephants, wolves, birds, fish, deer, whales — all dark and solid black, tightly crowded together. Moving right across the frame, the silhouettes become fewer, smaller, more faded and transparent, dissolving into pale grey ghosts. On the far right, only three or four barely-visible silhouettes remain, almost invisible against the white emptiness. The transition from full black to near-invisible happens gradually like ink fading in water. Vast empty white space dominates the right two-thirds of the image. Mood: silent extinction, irreversible absence, grief. Color palette: pure white background, deep solid black on left, dissolving pale grey towards right, no other colors. Rule of thirds composition. Minimalist. Photorealistic silhouettes. Absolutely no text, no words, no letters, no numbers, no chart elements, no axes, no labels, no legends anywhere in the image.
insight: Global wildlife populations have collapsed by an average of 73% since 1970 — a loss of nearly three-quarters of all wild animals in a single human lifetime.
